title: Eriostemon nudus
taxonomy: Eriostemon nudus is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family consists of approximately 160 genera and over 1,500 species of flowering plants. Eriostemon nudus is further classified within the genus Eriostemon, which is a group of Australian plants. The genus Eriostemon is part of the tribe Boronieae, within the subfamily Boronoideae. Eriostemon nudus's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Rutaceae family.

intro: Eriostemon nudus, commonly known as Yellow Bird's Eye, is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and distinctive, pungent aroma. Traditionally used in Australian bush medicine, Eriostemon nudus is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Eriostemon nudus include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Eriostemon nudus is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in traditional Australian remedies such as infusions, tinctures, and salves.
